key_themes: Iran War tensions resurge as Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, Brent crude tops $105/bbl
AI subscription anxiety reignited: IBM and ServiceNow earnings beats punished on slowing growth and geopolitical deal delays
Semiconductor analog upcycle confirmed: Texas Instruments Q1 blowout (+19% day) signals industrial and data center demand inflection

stocks.reason: Surged 19% to record high after Q1 2026 blowout: revenue $4.83B (+19% YoY) and EPS $1.68 both crushed estimates; CEO cited 90% data center revenue growth; multiple analyst upgrades from Stifel, TD Cowen, and Mizuho
Tumbled ~18% despite Q1 revenue beat ($3.77B vs $3.74B est); 75bps headwind to subscription growth from Middle East deal delays tied to Iran War; AI-disruption fears amplified by weak outlook optics

Fell nearly 8% after Q1 2026 beat (EPS beat by $0.10, revenue beat by $300M) failed to impress; revenue growth slowed to 9% from 12.2%; company offered no guidance raise, reigniting AI-disruption concerns for enterprise software
Soared 15-19% in after-hours trading after Q1 2026 earnings crushed expectations on revenue growth; marked a significant beat for the chipmaker amid its ongoing turnaround effort
Rallied 15% after Q1 results beat expectations, driven by strength in regional casinos and interactive/online gambling segments
Jumped 13%+ after Q1 beat and raised full-year sales guidance to $16.9B–$17.4B range, signaling robust infrastructure and construction demand
Shares surged 8.2% after posting a Q1 2026 quarterly profit beat, rebounding from prior weakness in telecom infrastructure spending
Rose 7.5% to $52.02 as the market continued rewarding its pivot from Bitcoin mining toward AI data center operations; ongoing narrative of GPU compute monetization
Popped 7% after Q1 2026 EPS of $0.43 beat the $0.39 consensus; results highlighted in Bloomberg premarket movers list
Fell ~3% in premarket and regular session despite Q1 EPS beat; revenue came in below expectations, disappointing investors who had anticipated a full beat
Dropped ~4% after Q1 2026 earnings and revenue both missed Wall Street forecasts, a notable stumble for the defense giant despite elevated geopolitical tensions from the Iran War

Volatile session: Q1 delivered a top- and bottom-line beat with automotive business better than feared, but shares reversed after CEO Musk announced plans to increase capital investments to over $25B for robotics pivot
Beat Q1 earnings and revenue forecasts but cut full-year 2026 earnings outlook citing surging oil prices driven by the Iran War and Strait of Hormuz disruptions
Slipped 7% in after-hours trading after Q1 adjusted EPS of $1.60 missed the $1.73 consensus; revenue of $997.4M also fell short of the expected $1B
JPMorgan downgraded to Underweight from Neutral citing unsustainable valuation not supported by fundamentals; new price target $165
